source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test
title: CAE C1 Advanced Official Practice Test 1

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-8, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).
sections.passageHtml:
The Secret Life of Trees

For many years, scientists believed that trees were solitary organisms that competed for resources like sunlight and water. However, recent research has (0) ______ a fascinating network of cooperation underneath the forest floor. Trees actually communicate and share resources with one another (1) ______ a complex network of fungal threads known as mycorrhizae. This network is so extensive that it has been (2) ______ the 'Wood Wide Web'. Through it, trees can send warning signals about pests, share nutrients with sick neighbors, and even (3) ______ their offspring. Oldest trees, often called 'mother trees', act as central hubs, (4) ______ resources to younger saplings. When a mother tree is injured, she may (5) ______ her resources to her neighbors to ensure the survival of the forest community. This discovery has completely (6) ______ our understanding of forestry and ecology, proving that cooperation, rather than competition, is (7) ______ to a healthy forest ecosystem. We must now (8) ______ forests as complex superorganisms that need collective protection.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B, C or D.

questions.explanation: Chọn B - 'through'. Giới từ 'through' chỉ phương tiện hoặc cách thức hoạt động ('through a complex network' - thông qua một mạng lưới phức tạp). Các từ khác như 'via' cũng chỉ cách thức nhưng 'through' là collocation tự nhiên nhất trong ngữ cảnh này. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: B. through. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.explanation: Chọn D - 'dubbed'. Động từ 'dub' mang nghĩa là phong cho danh hiệu, đặt biệt hiệu ('been dubbed the Wood Wide Web'). Các từ khác không hợp nghĩa: 'called' không dùng với mạo từ/cách cấu trúc bị động này, 'named' thường là đặt tên chính thức. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. dubbed. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.explanation: Chọn A - 'nourish'. 'Nourish' nghĩa là nuôi dưỡng, cung cấp chất dinh dưỡng để lớn lên, phù hợp với 'offspring' (con non/cây con). Các từ khác như 'feed' hoặc 'support' ít trang trọng và không đi kèm cấu trúc sinh học này. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. nourish. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.explanation: Chọn C - 'allocating'. 'Allocate' nghĩa là phân bổ, phân phối nguồn lực có mục đích ('allocating resources to younger saplings'). Các phương án khác như 'giving' quá thông dụng, 'distributing' thiếu sắc thái phân chia có chủ đích. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. allocating. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.explanation: Chọn B - 'transfer'. 'Transfer' nghĩa là chuyển nhượng, di chuyển tài sản/nguồn lực từ đối tượng này sang đối tượng khác ('transfer her resources to her neighbors'). Các từ khác không hợp giới từ hoặc sắc thái nghĩa. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: B. transfer. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.explanation: Chọn A - 'revolutionized'. Cụm từ 'completely revolutionized our understanding' (cách mạng hóa hoàn toàn hiểu biết của chúng ta) là một collocation cực mạnh và phổ biến trong văn phong học thuật. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. revolutionized. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.explanation: Chọn C - 'vital'. Tính từ 'vital to/for something' có nghĩa là sống còn, tối quan trọng đối với sự phát triển của hệ sinh thái. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context. Language rule: Part 1 tests lexical choice, collocation, phrasal verbs and register; the correct option must fit both meaning and pattern.
questions.answerLocation: C. vital. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.explanation: Chọn D - 'regard'. Cấu trúc 'regard something as something' (xem cái gì như là cái gì) đi kèm rất chuẩn với giới từ 'as' ở phía sau ('regard forests as complex superorganisms'). Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. regard. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

sections.title: Part 2: Open Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 9-16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each space. Use only one word in each space. There is an example at the beginning (0).
sections.passageHtml:
The Invention of the Typewriter

The typewriter, (0) ______ which many modern computers would not exist, was invented in the 19th century. (9) ______ its early days, the typewriter was seen as a luxury item rather than an essential tool. However, within a few decades, it (10) ______ become a staple in offices all over the world, transforming the nature of business and communication. The typewriter was particularly revolutionary in (11) ______ it opened up new employment opportunities for women, who quickly dominated the field of typing. Before the typewriter's rise, office work was almost exclusively a male domain. But (12) ______ to the sudden demand for fast, efficient typing, thousands of young women entered the workforce. (13) ______ only did the typewriter alter the social makeup of offices, but it also changed the way people composed literature. Many famous authors, including Mark Twain, were (14) ______ the first to embrace this technology, claiming it allowed (15) ______ to write much faster and with greater clarity. Despite the rise of digital computers, many people still collect old mechanical typewriters, fascinated by (16) ______ unique sound and history.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct word in each gap in CAPITAL letters.

acceptedAnswers: In its early days, the typewriter
Điền 'IN'. Trạng ngữ chỉ thời gian phổ biến 'In its early days' Trong những ngày đầu mới ra mắt.
Điền 'IN'. Trạng ngữ chỉ thời gian phổ biến 'In its early days' .
questions.explanation: Điền 'IN'. Trạng ngữ chỉ thời gian phổ biến 'In its early days' (Trong những ngày đầu mới ra mắt). Method: complete the gap with "IN" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: open cloze usually tests grammar words such as articles, prepositions, auxiliaries, pronouns, linkers and fixed phrases.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the correct word in each gap in CAPITAL letters. Gap (9):. Correct completion: IN.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 2: Open Cloze

acceptedAnswers: it had become a staple in
questions.explanation: Điền 'HAD'. Trợ động từ để tạo thành thì quá khứ hoàn thành 'it had become' để diễn tả một sự việc đã xảy ra và hoàn tất trước một mốc thời gian trong quá khứ. Method: complete the gap with "HAD" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the correct word in each gap in CAPITAL letters. Gap (10):. Correct completion: HAD.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 2: Open Cloze

acceptedAnswers: revolutionary in that it opened up new
Điền 'THAT'. Cấu trúc chỉ nguyên nhân
questions.explanation: Điền 'THAT'. Cấu trúc chỉ nguyên nhân/sự việc 'in that' có nghĩa là 'ở chỗ, bởi vì'. Mệnh đề phía sau giải thích rõ hơn cho sự mang tính cách mạng của máy đánh chữ. Method: complete the gap with "THAT" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: revolutionary in that it opened up new
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 2: Open Cloze

acceptedAnswers: But due to the sudden demand for
questions.explanation: Điền 'DUE'. Một phần của cụm giới từ chỉ nguyên nhân 'due to' (bởi vì, nhờ vào). Method: complete the gap with "DUE" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the correct word in each gap in CAPITAL letters. Gap (12):. Correct completion: DUE.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 2: Open Cloze

acceptedAnswers: Not only did the typewriter alter the social
Điền 'NOT'. Cấu trúc đảo ngữ kết hợp phủ định nhấn mạnh 'Not only did ... but it also ...' .
questions.explanation: Điền 'NOT'. Cấu trúc đảo ngữ kết hợp phủ định nhấn mạnh 'Not only did ... but it also ...' (Không những... mà còn...). Method: complete the gap with "NOT" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Not only did the typewriter alter the social
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 2: Open Cloze

acceptedAnswers: were among the first to embrace this
Điền 'AMONG'. Giới từ chỉ tập hợp 'among the first' ứng dụng công nghệ này.
questions.explanation: Điền 'AMONG'. Giới từ chỉ tập hợp 'among the first' (nằm trong số những người đầu tiên) ứng dụng công nghệ này. Method: complete the gap with "AMONG" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: were among the first to embrace this
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 2: Open Cloze

acceptedAnswers: claiming it allowed them to write
questions.explanation: Điền 'THEM'. Đại từ tân ngữ chỉ số nhiều 'allowed them to write' (cho phép họ viết) thay thế cho danh từ 'Many famous authors'. Method: complete the gap with "THEM" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: open cloze usually tests grammar words such as articles, prepositions, auxiliaries, pronouns, linkers and fixed phrases.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the correct word in each gap in CAPITAL letters. Gap (15):. Correct completion: THEM.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 2: Open Cloze

acceptedAnswers: fascinated by its unique sound and history.
Điền 'ITS'. Tính từ sở hữu chỉ vật số ít 'its unique sound' để chỉ chiếc máy đánh chữ cơ học.
questions.explanation: Điền 'ITS'. Tính từ sở hữu chỉ vật số ít 'its unique sound' (âm thanh đặc trưng của nó) để chỉ chiếc máy đánh chữ cơ học. Method: complete the gap with "ITS" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: open cloze usually tests grammar words such as articles, prepositions, auxiliaries, pronouns, linkers and fixed phrases.
questions.answerLocation: fascinated by its unique sound and history.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 2: Open Cloze

sections.title: Part 3: Word Formation
sections.instructions: For questions 17-24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the space in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).
sections.passageHtml:
The Rise of E-Commerce

The internet has revolutionized the retail sector, leading to the (0) ______ rise of online shopping. Today, e-commerce has made purchasing goods extremely (17) ______ for consumers globally. With a simple tap on a screen, products can be delivered to one's doorstep with remarkable (18) ______. This virtual marketplace has had a deep impact on (19) ______ brick-and-mortar stores, many of which have struggled to survive. To stay competitive, traditional retailers must develop (20) ______ digital marketing strategies and improve customer engagement. Furthermore, online platforms must prioritize the (21) ______ of transaction data to protect customer privacy and prevent fraud. Any security breach can result in a (22) ______ loss of trust in the brand. Therefore, developers are constantly updating encryption protocols to ensure that all financial transactions remain (23) ______ safe. Despite these security concerns, the (24) ______ of online shopping is expected to grow as technology continues to evolve, making virtual retail even more seamless.

questionGroups.instruction: Form the correct word from the prompt and write it in the gap.

acceptedAnswers: Biến đổi tính từ 'CONVENIENT'. Vị trí sau cấu trúc 'make + object + adjective' .
questions.explanation: Biến đổi tính từ 'CONVENIENT'. Vị trí sau cấu trúc 'make + object + adjective' ('made purchasing goods extremely convenient'). Method: complete the gap with "CONVENIENT" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: made purchasing goods extremely convenient
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 3: Word Formation

acceptedAnswers: delivered to one's doorstep with remarkable swiftness
questions.explanation: Biến đổi danh từ 'SWIFTNESS'. Sau giới từ 'with' và tính từ 'remarkable', chúng ta cần danh từ bổ nghĩa ('with remarkable swiftness' - với tốc độ nhanh đáng kể). Method: complete the gap with "SWIFTNESS" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: delivered to one's doorstep with remarkable swiftness
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 3: Word Formation

questions.explanation: Biến đổi tính từ 'TRADITIONAL'. Đứng trước cụm danh từ 'brick-and-mortar stores' để bổ nghĩa ('traditional brick-and-mortar stores' - cửa hàng vật lý truyền thống). Method: complete the gap with "TRADITIONAL" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: word formation requires the correct part of speech, affix, spelling and sometimes negative/positive meaning.
questions.answerLocation: impact on traditional brick-and-mortar stores
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 3: Word Formation

acceptedAnswers: traditional retailers must develop innovative digital
questions.explanation: Biến đổi tính từ 'INNOVATIVE'. Cần tính từ đứng trước danh từ 'digital marketing strategies' ('develop innovative digital marketing strategies' - các chiến lược marketing sáng tạo/đổi mới). Method: complete the gap with "INNOVATIVE" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: word formation requires the correct part of speech, affix, spelling and sometimes negative/positive meaning.
questions.answerLocation: traditional retailers must develop innovative digital
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 3: Word Formation

acceptedAnswers: must prioritize the security of transaction data
questions.explanation: Biến đổi danh từ 'SECURITY'. Vị trí sau mạo từ xác định 'the' và trước giới từ 'of' cần danh từ làm chủ thể ('the security of transaction data' - sự bảo mật của dữ liệu giao dịch). Method: complete the gap with "SECURITY" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: must prioritize the security of transaction data
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 3: Word Formation

acceptedAnswers: result in a catastrophic loss of trust
questions.explanation: Biến đổi tính từ 'CATASTROPHIC'. Đứng trước bổ nghĩa cho danh từ 'loss' ('result in a catastrophic loss of trust' - kết quả là sự suy giảm niềm tin mang tính thảm họa). Method: complete the gap with "CATASTROPHIC" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: word formation requires the correct part of speech, affix, spelling and sometimes negative/positive meaning.
questions.answerLocation: result in a catastrophic loss of trust
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 3: Word Formation

questions.explanation: Biến đổi trạng từ 'EXCLUSIVELY'. Trạng từ đứng trước tính từ 'safe' để bổ nghĩa biểu đạt mức độ ('remain exclusively safe' - hoàn toàn/độc quyền an toàn). Method: complete the gap with "EXCLUSIVELY" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: word formation requires the correct part of speech, affix, spelling and sometimes negative/positive meaning.
questions.answerLocation: financial transactions remain exclusively safe.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 3: Word Formation

acceptedAnswers: the popularity of online shopping is expected
Biến đổi danh từ 'POPULARITY'. Đứng sau mạo từ 'the' làm chủ ngữ chính của mệnh đề .
questions.explanation: Biến đổi danh từ 'POPULARITY'. Đứng sau mạo từ 'the' làm chủ ngữ chính của mệnh đề ('the popularity of online shopping' - sự phổ biến của mua sắm trực tuyến). Method: complete the gap with "POPULARITY" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: the popularity of online shopping is expected
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 3: Word Formation

sections.title: Part 4: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: For questions 25-30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).
sections.passageHtml:
Example:

0 It is too cold for us to go swimming today.

IF

We could go swimming today if it were not so cold.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the missing words in CAPITAL LETTERS.

questions.instruction: Q25. I regret not buying that book when I had the chance. (WISH) -> I ______ that book when I had the chance.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WISH I HAD BOUGHT. Cấu trúc câu ao ước trái thực tế trong quá khứ: 'S + wish + S + had + P2' (Tôi ước gì đã mua...). Method: complete the gap with "WISH I HAD BOUGHT" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the missing words in CAPITAL LETTERS. Q25. I regret not buying that book when I had the chance. (WISH) -> I ___.... Correct completion: WISH I HAD BOUGHT.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 4: Key Word Transformation

questions.instruction: Q26. John had difficulty understanding the teacher's explanation. (HARD) -> John found ______ the teacher's explanation.
acceptedAnswers: IT HARD TO UNDERSTAND. Cấu trúc 'find + it + adj + to-V' (John nhận thấy thật khó để hiểu...).
IT HARD TO UNDERSTAND. Cấu trúc 'find + it + adj + to-V' John nhận thấy thật khó để hiểu....
IT HARD TO UNDERSTAND. Cấu trúc 'find + it + adj + to-V' .
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IT HARD TO UNDERSTAND. Cấu trúc 'find + it + adj + to-V' (John nhận thấy thật khó để hiểu...). Method: complete the gap with "IT HARD TO UNDERSTAND" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the missing words in CAPITAL LETTERS. Q26. John had difficulty understanding the teacher's explanation. (HARD) .... Correct completion: IT HARD TO UNDERSTAND.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 4: Key Word Transformation

questions.instruction: Q27. The heavy rain prevented the match from taking place. (CALL) -> The match had ______ of the heavy rain.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: TO BE CALLED OFF BECAUSE. Phrasal verb 'call off' nghĩa là hủy bỏ, chuyển sang bị động quá khứ đơn 'had to be called off' đi kèm liên từ 'because' hoặc 'on account of'. Method: complete the gap with "TO BE CALLED OFF BECAUSE" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: key word transformation keeps the key word unchanged while preserving meaning through grammar pattern and lexical control.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the missing words in CAPITAL LETTERS. Q27. The heavy rain prevented the match from taking place. (CALL) -> The .... Correct completion: TO BE CALLED OFF BECAUSE.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 4: Key Word Transformation

questions.instruction: Q28. It is highly likely that the team will win the championship. (CHANCE) -> The team has ______ winning the championship.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: A HIGH CHANCE OF / EVERY CHANCE OF. Cấu trúc danh từ chỉ khả năng: 'have a high/good chance of doing something'. Method: complete the gap with "A HIGH CHANCE OF" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the missing words in CAPITAL LETTERS. Q28. It is highly likely that the team will win the championship. (CHANCE.... Correct completion: A HIGH CHANCE OF.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 4: Key Word Transformation

questions.instruction: Q29. 'I am sorry I arrived so late,' said Sarah to the host. (APOLOGIZED) -> Sarah ______ late.
acceptedAnswers: APOLOGIZED TO THE HOST FOR ARRIVING
APOLOGISED TO THE HOST FOR ARRIVING
questions.correctAnswer: APOLOGIZED TO THE HOST FOR ARRIVING
questions.explanation: Đáp án: APOLOGIZED TO THE HOST FOR ARRIVING. Động từ phản hồi: 'apologize to somebody for doing something' (Xin lỗi ai vì đã làm gì). Method: complete the gap with "APOLOGIZED TO THE HOST FOR ARRIVING" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: key word transformation keeps the key word unchanged while preserving meaning through grammar pattern and lexical control.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the missing words in CAPITAL LETTERS. Q29. 'I am sorry I arrived so late,' said Sarah to the host. (APOLOGIZED).... Correct completion: APOLOGIZED TO THE HOST FOR ARRIVING.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 4: Key Word Transformation

questions.instruction: Q30. You should check the engine oil before starting a long trip. (WORTH) -> It ______ the engine oil before starting a long trip.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IS WORTH CHECKING. Cấu trúc khuyên nghị thông dụng: 'It is worth + V-ing' (Rất đáng/Nên làm gì). Method: complete the gap with "IS WORTH CHECKING" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the missing words in CAPITAL LETTERS. Q30. You should check the engine oil before starting a long trip. (WORTH).... Correct completion: IS WORTH CHECKING.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 4: Key Word Transformation

sections.title: Part 5: Multiple Choice Reading
sections.instructions: For questions 31-36, read the article below and choose the correct answer (A, B, C or D).
sections.passageHtml:
The Evolution of Human Intelligence

How did human beings develop such advanced cognitive abilities? For decades, evolutionary biologists debated whether our brains expanded due to ecological challenges—like finding food and making tools—or social challenges, such as living in complex groups. Recently, researchers have increasingly favored the 'Social Brain Hypothesis,' which argues that the intense cognitive demands of managing social networks drove our intellectual evolution.

According to this hypothesis, living in larger groups offers enormous benefits, such as protection from predators and pooled resources, but it also creates high levels of interpersonal conflict. To survive, individuals had to remember complex relationships, identify allies from rivals, and master deceptive strategies to gain resources without triggering aggressive retaliation. This constant psychological negotiation necessitated a massive expansion of the neocortex—the brain region responsible for planning and logical reasoning.

Furthermore, human brains are incredibly expensive to run, consuming up to 20% of the body's energy while accounting for only 2% of its weight. This 'energy budget constraint' suggests that our ancestors could only afford to grow larger brains by shifting to high-calorie diets, such as meat, and discovering cooking, which dramatically reduced the time and energy needed to digest raw food. Therefore, our cognitive leap forward was not just a biological miracle, but a highly coordinated evolutionary tradeoff between diet, social dynamics, and physiological architecture.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: Q31. According to the first paragraph, what did evolutionary biologists debate?
options: A. Whether brain size is linked to physical body weight.
B. Whether social dynamics or environmental challenges drove brain expansion.
C. Whether early humans developed tools before or after group living.
D. Whether ecological resources were abundant in early human history.
questions.explanation: Chọn B. Đoạn 1 nêu rõ các nhà sinh học tiến hóa tranh luận liệu bộ não mở rộng do thử thách sinh thái ('ecological challenges') hay thử thách xã hội ('social challenges'). Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: B. Whether social dynamics or environmental challenges drove brain expansion.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 5: Multiple Choice Reading

questions.instruction: Q32. What does the Social Brain Hypothesis argue?
options: A. That humans became smarter by isolating themselves in small, cooperative groups.
B. That language was the only catalyst for biological brain development.
C. That managing complex social networks stimulated our intellectual growth.
D. That predator pressure alone drove early cognitive development.
questions.explanation: Chọn C. Đoạn 1 nêu rõ thuyết này lập luận rằng yêu cầu nhận thức cao của việc quản lý các mạng lưới xã hội đã thúc đẩy tiến hóa trí tuệ ('cognitive demands of managing social networks drove our intellectual evolution'). Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. That managing complex social networks stimulated our intellectual growth.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 5: Multiple Choice Reading

questions.instruction: Q33. According to the second paragraph, what advantage did early humans gain from larger groups?
options: A. Enhanced safety from predators and shared community resources.
B. Immediate eradication of internal conflicts.
C. Lower energy consumption rates for individual members.
questions.explanation: Chọn A. Đoạn 2 nêu 'living in larger groups offers enormous benefits, such as protection from predators and pooled resources' (an toàn trước thú dữ và chia sẻ tài nguyên). Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. Enhanced safety from predators and shared community resources.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 5: Multiple Choice Reading

questions.instruction: Q34. Why was the expansion of the neocortex crucial?
options: A. It reduced physical sleep requirements.
B. It allowed direct communication with other animal species.
C. It immediately halted all internal conflicts within human groups.
D. It enabled strategic planning and management of intense group politics.
questions.explanation: Chọn D. Đoạn 2 giải thích việc thương thảo tâm lý liên tục (quản lý quan hệ, đồng minh, mưu mẹo...) đòi hỏi sự phát triển của neocortex để lập kế hoạch và lập luận ('planning and logical reasoning'). Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. It enabled strategic planning and management of intense group politics.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 5: Multiple Choice Reading

questions.instruction: Q35. What is the significance of the human brain's energy consumption?
options: A. It requires humans to sleep for more than half the day.
B. It is extremely low compared to other mammals.
C. It consumes 20% of our energy while being only 2% of our body weight.
D. It restricts early humans from physical activities.
questions.explanation: Chọn C. Đoạn 3 chỉ ra 'consuming up to 20% of the body's energy while accounting for only 2% of its weight' (tiêu thụ 20% năng lượng cơ thể mặc dù chỉ chiếm 2% trọng lượng). Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. It consumes 20% of our energy while being only 2% of our body weight.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 5: Multiple Choice Reading

questions.instruction: Q36. How did our ancestors overcome the 'energy budget constraint' to support larger brains?
options: A. By switching to high-calorie diets like meat and discovering cooking.
B. By decreasing their physical movement and resting more.
C. By eating raw vegetation throughout the day.
D. By hunting smaller insects rather than large animals.
questions.explanation: Chọn A. Đoạn 3 giải thích tổ tiên vượt qua rào cản năng lượng bằng cách đổi sang chế độ ăn nhiều calo như thịt ('high-calorie diets, such as meat') và nấu chín thức ăn ('cooking'). Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. By switching to high-calorie diets like meat and discovering cooking.. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 5: Multiple Choice Reading

sections.title: Part 6: Cross-Text Multiple Matching
sections.instructions: For questions 37-40, read the four academic perspectives (A-D) on the paradox of technology in education and choose the correct perspective that matches each statement. You may choose a perspective more than once.
sections.passageHtml:
The Paradox of Technology in Education

A. Dr. Adrian Vance (Educational Psychologist)
The rapid integration of digital platforms in schools has triggered a severe decline in student attention spans and deep text comprehension. While advocates argue that tablets and interactive apps stimulate engagement, cognitive science demonstrates that screen-based learning encourages passive scrolling and superficial information processing. Furthermore, corporate tech platforms are heavily optimized to maximize engagement, converting classrooms into commercial spaces. This commercial framing has forced educators to walk a tightrope, caught between satisfying artificial metrics and trying to preserve their pedagogical integrity. Genuine breakthroughs are born when we unplug and return to tactile, deep reading experiences.

B. Dr. Clara Sterling (Sociologist of Education)
It is impossible to deny that digital networks have democratized educational access. Marginalized students and rural classrooms can now study open-source materials from the world's finest universities. However, this democratization is a double-edged sword. It has led to a highly fragmented learning environment where the boundary between rigorous instruction and simple online entertainment is hopelessly blurred. Because anyone can upload a tutorial, digital platforms promote superficial, impressionistic experiences designed to capture short attention spans. We are left with an educational culture that values instant viral consumption over deep, enduring mastery, making it exceptionally difficult for students to build critical thinking.

C. Dr. Julian Thorne (Learning Technologist)
Skepticism toward educational technology is reminiscent of historical anxieties surrounding the invention of the printing press. Every major leap in technology disrupts cognitive habits. Rather than pathologizing screen learning, we must adapt our methodologies.sleek, interactive digital interfaces offer personalized, adaptive pathways that cater to different learning speeds—an equity benefit that brick-and-mortar classrooms cannot achieve. Furthermore, tech platforms do not replace traditional teaching; they enhance it. By automating routine administrative tasks and grading, teachers are actually freed to engage in more visceral, one-on-one relationships with their students.

D. Prof. Eleanor Brooks (Cognitive Scientist)
Our brain's processing of information is fundamentally shaped by the medium of consumption. While digital tools have their place, they pose substantial risks to deep reading comprehension. Screen-based learning is highly distracting, training the brain to demand constant visual transitions. Consequently, students find it difficult to read long texts, losing the capacity for sustained concentration. In their efforts to communicate with a screen-weary public, even serious publishers are simplifying textbook materials, compromising academic rigor. We must build robust 'fault-tolerant' learning environments that actively limit digital screen time and protect traditional cognitive practices.

questionGroups.instruction: Select the correct letter A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: Q37. Which academic shares a similar view to Dr. Adrian Vance regarding the negative impact of screens on deep reading comprehension?
questions.explanation: Chọn D - Prof. Eleanor Brooks. Cả Dr. Adrian Vance (A) và Prof. Eleanor Brooks (D) đều đồng thuận rằng học qua màn hình làm suy giảm khả năng đọc sâu và hiểu văn bản chi tiết. Vance viết: 'severe decline in student attention spans and deep text comprehension'. Brooks viết: 'they pose substantial risks to deep reading comprehension'. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. Answer-key option. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 6: Cross-Text Multiple Matching

questions.instruction: Q38. Which academic expresses a different view from the others by highlighting the positive personalization and equity benefits of educational technology?
questions.explanation: Chọn C - Dr. Julian Thorne. Khác với các quan điểm lo âu của A, B và D về công nghệ, Thorne (C) nhấn mạnh giá trị tích cực về mặt cá nhân hóa lộ trình học và tính công bằng: 'sleek, interactive digital interfaces offer personalized, adaptive pathways ... an equity benefit'. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. Answer-key option. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 6: Cross-Text Multiple Matching

questions.instruction: Q39. Which academic shares a concern with Dr. Clara Sterling that online platforms have blurred the line between rigorous education and shallow entertainment?
questions.explanation: Chọn A - Dr. Adrian Vance. Cả Sterling (B) và Vance (A) đều lo lắng về việc biến không gian giáo dục thành nơi thương mại/giải trí nông cạn. Sterling nói về việc mờ nhạt ranh giới giữa học thuật và giải trí ('rigorous instruction and simple online entertainment is hopelessly blurred'). Vance nói về việc thương mại hóa biến lớp học thành không gian quảng cáo ('converting classrooms into commercial spaces'). Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. Answer-key option. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 6: Cross-Text Multiple Matching

questions.explanation: Chọn C - Dr. Julian Thorne. Thorne viết: 'By automating routine administrative tasks and grading, teachers are actually freed to engage in more visceral, one-on-one relationships'. Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.

sections.title: Part 7: Gapped Text
sections.instructions: For questions 41-46, read the text below from which six paragraphs have been removed. Choose from paragraphs A-G the one which fits each gap. There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
sections.passageHtml:
The Forgotten Library

Deep in the mountains of Europe lies the town of Altdorf, entirely unremarkable except for a massive stone tower that stands on its eastern cliff. For centuries, the townspeople believed the tower was an ancient defensive fort, long abandoned and empty. However, last summer, a young historian named Clara made an extraordinary discovery inside.

[Gap 41]

After clearing away thick layers of dust and cobwebs, Clara was astonished to find thousands of leather-bound manuscripts lining the stone walls. These were not military records; they were a vast collection of lost literature, classical science texts, and historical journals dating back to the Middle Ages. The library had apparently been sealed off to protect it from war.

[Gap 42]

Among the manuscripts, Clara discovered a handwritten ledger containing the names of the scholars who had worked in the library. This ledger confirmed that the library was established by a secret society of philosophers who sought to preserve human knowledge during times of extreme political instability. They believed that if knowledge was kept in one place, it would remain safe.

[Gap 43]

However, the ledger also hinted at a darker chapter in the library's history. According to the final entries, a massive political crisis arose in the region, forcing the scholars to abandon the tower in a hurry. They sealed the massive iron doors and hid the keys, hoping that one day someone worthy would return to reclaim the treasure.

[Gap 44]

News of Clara's discovery spread like wildfire throughout the academic world, attracting historians and archaeologists from every corner of the globe. A team from the university arrived to help categorize and restore the fragile texts, using advanced digital scanning technology to preserve the contents before the ancient paper decayed completely.

[Gap 45]

Yet, the Altdorf library is more than just a historical archive. The discovery has brought a sudden wave of tourism to the sleepy town, transforming its economy overnight. Local cafes and guesthouses are packed with visitors, and the town council is planning to build a modern museum at the foot of the cliff to showcase digital replicas of the manuscripts.

[Gap 46]

As the restoration continues, scholars realize that the Altdorf collection will completely rewrite the history of science and philosophy in early Europe. The secret society succeeded beyond their wildest dreams: they kept human knowledge safe, waiting in the dark for centuries until the world was finally ready to receive it once more.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter A-G for each gap.

paragraphOptions.label: A
paragraphOptions.text: This sudden influx of academics and curious travelers has created a lively atmosphere, though some elderly townspeople worry that Altdorf will lose its quiet, traditional charm.

paragraphOptions.label: B
paragraphOptions.text: By examining the structural masonry of the tower, Clara found a hidden lever behind a stone carving that opened a secret door leading down into a subterranean vault.

paragraphOptions.label: C
paragraphOptions.text: To keep the library safe from invaders, the scholars created a highly secure system of codes and locks that protected the primary vault from unauthorized entry.

paragraphOptions.label: D
paragraphOptions.text: This painstaking process of scanning was absolutely critical, as many of the oldest leather-bound books were so fragile that the simple act of turning a page could cause it to crumble.

paragraphOptions.label: E
paragraphOptions.text: These ancient documents were exceptionally well-preserved, protected by the cool, dry air of the underground vault and the thick stone walls that shielded them from the elements.

paragraphOptions.label: F
paragraphOptions.text: This tragic flight must have been incredibly traumatic, as several valuable manuscripts were left scattered on the floor, abandoned in their desperate haste to escape.

paragraphOptions.label: G
paragraphOptions.text: This alternative theory was dismissed by major researchers, who argued that the tower was built solely for astronomical observation.

questions.explanation: Chọn B. Đoạn B mô tả cách Clara tìm ra lối đi bí mật bằng đòn bẩy ẩn ('hidden lever behind a stone carving that opened a secret door'), nối tiếp hoàn hảo câu trước kể về phát hiện của cô và câu sau miêu tả căn phòng phủ đầy bụi và mạng nhện. Method: match by paraphrase and discourse logic, then eliminate partial matches. Trap: a repeated keyword alone is not enough; check reference, contrast and the full claim.
questions.answerLocation: The Forgotten Library Deep in the mountains of Europe lies the town of Altdorf, entirely unremarkable except for a massive stone tower that stands on its eastern cliff.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 7: Gapped Text

questions.explanation: Chọn E. Đoạn E mô tả tình trạng bảo quản của các tài liệu ('ancient documents were exceptionally well-preserved'), làm rõ lý do tại sao Clara có thể đọc được các bản thảo bằng da từ thời Trung Cổ. Method: match by paraphrase and discourse logic, then eliminate partial matches. Trap: a repeated keyword alone is not enough; check reference, contrast and the full claim.
questions.answerLocation: The Forgotten Library Deep in the mountains of Europe lies the town of Altdorf, entirely unremarkable except for a massive stone tower that stands on its eastern cliff.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 7: Gapped Text

questions.explanation: Chọn C. Đoạn C liên quan đến 'highly secure system of codes and locks' (hệ thống khóa bảo mật), giải thích cách hội triết học bí mật bảo vệ tri thức khỏi kẻ thù. Method: match by paraphrase and discourse logic, then eliminate partial matches. Trap: a repeated keyword alone is not enough; check reference, contrast and the full claim.
questions.answerLocation: highly secure system of codes and locks
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 7: Gapped Text

questions.explanation: Chọn F. Đoạn F mô tả cuộc chạy trốn khẩn cấp ('tragic flight must have been incredibly traumatic') giải thích vì sao sổ đăng ký ghi nhận các học giả phải bỏ tháp vội vàng. Method: match by paraphrase and discourse logic, then eliminate partial matches. Trap: a repeated keyword alone is not enough; check reference, contrast and the full claim.
questions.answerLocation: The Forgotten Library Deep in the mountains of Europe lies the town of Altdorf, entirely unremarkable except for a massive stone tower that stands on its eastern cliff.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 7: Gapped Text

questions.explanation: Chọn D. Đoạn D bổ sung quá trình scan tài liệu ('painstaking process of scanning'), làm rõ việc sử dụng công nghệ số hóa đã nêu ở đoạn trước. Method: match by paraphrase and discourse logic, then eliminate partial matches. Trap: a repeated keyword alone is not enough; check reference, contrast and the full claim.
questions.answerLocation: The Forgotten Library Deep in the mountains of Europe lies the town of Altdorf, entirely unremarkable except for a massive stone tower that stands on its eastern cliff.
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 7: Gapped Text

questions.explanation: Chọn A. Đoạn A mô tả làn sóng du lịch ('influx of academics and curious travelers') liên hệ trực tiếp đến các quán cafe, nhà nghỉ chật kín khách và phát triển kinh tế ở Altdorf. Method: match by paraphrase and discourse logic, then eliminate partial matches. Trap: a repeated keyword alone is not enough; check reference, contrast and the full claim.
questions.answerLocation: influx of academics and curious travelers has created a lively atmosphere
questions.source: Cambridge English Advanced Official Practice Test, Part 7: Gapped Text

sections.title: Part 8: Multiple Matching
sections.instructions: For questions 47-56, read the five reviews of scientific books (A-E) below and choose which book reviews matches each statement.
sections.passageHtml:
Book Reviews: Scientific Non-Fiction

A. Review of 'The Quantum Mind' by Dr. Adrian Sterling
This volume explores the cutting-edge intersections of quantum physics and neuroscience, arguing that consciousness itself might be a quantum phenomenon. Sterling writes with a lucid, highly accessible style that manages to demystify complex formulas. However, the book has a severe flaw: it tends to oversimplify biological structures, presenting hypothetical models as established scientific facts. Despite this, it is an engaging, provocative read that challenges conventional paradigms.

B. Review of 'The Hidden Forest' by Clara Thorne
Thorne's book is a poetic, beautifully illustrated exploration of forest ecology. She details how trees cooperate through fungal networks, sharing nutrients and warning signals. While the scientific evidence she presents is rigorous, her tone is occasionally overly sentimental, attributing human emotions to plants. However, the book excels in making ecological science deeply relatable, encouraging readers to see the natural world as a highly interconnected community.

C. Review of 'Digital Shadows' by Julian Vance
Vance provides a sobering, dark analysis of the social media landscape. He argues that corporate algorithms have systematically commodified human attention, creating severe cognitive deficits in modern users. The book is highly academic, filled with empirical data and sociological theories. While it is a challenging read, it is an essential volume for anyone wishing to understand the political economy of the internet and its impacts on human psychology.

D. Review of 'The Genetic Code' by Eleanor Brooks
Brooks delivers a masterclass in science writing, tracing the history of genetic research from Mendel to CRISPR. She manage to balance high-level scientific explanations with compelling human stories about the researchers themselves. Her prose is elegant and precise, avoiding both technical jargon and oversimplification. The only downside is that the book is extremely dense, requiring intense concentration from the reader.

E. Review of 'Fault Tolerant Life' by Arthur Pendelton
Pendelton explores the fascinating biology of extreme organisms, such as tardigrades and deep-sea bacteria, that survive in uninhabitable conditions. He argues that nature's ultimate secret is 'fault tolerance'—building systems that can adapt and survive failures. The book is highly practical, suggesting how these biological strategies can be applied to human technology and society. It is an inspiring, highly original volume.

questionGroups.instruction: Select the correct letter A, B, C, D or E.

Method: match by paraphrase and discourse logic, then eliminate partial matches. Trap: a repeated keyword alone is not enough; check reference, contrast and the full claim.
